name: claudeteam
description: Real-time dashboard for orchestrated Claude Code agent teams.

claudeteam.rosterPath.description: Override path to teams.yaml roster file. Empty string uses the default per-project location (<workspace>/.claude/teams.yaml), falling back to the global location (~/.claudeteam/teams.yaml) if no per-project roster is present.

claudeteam.pollIntervalMs.description: How often (in milliseconds) to poll ~/.claude/ for agent state updates. Default 2000ms tuned in M4-04 — see team/felix-dev/m4-04-cadence-measurement.md for methodology and raw numbers. Lowering below 2000ms is wasteful (Claude Code JSONL files flush in 2–56s bursts per data-sources.md §3 — sub-2s polling sees no new data). Raising to 3000ms+ saves negligible CPU and adds visible activity-line lag.

claudeteam.rosterPollIntervalMs.description: Fallback polling cadence (in milliseconds) for the roster YAML watcher. 0 = OFF (rely on VS Code's FileSystemWatcher). Set to a positive value (e.g. 5000) only if the FileSystemWatcher proves unreliable on your platform (some WSL paths, network drives). Values below 250 are clamped to the 250ms debounce floor — anything tighter would fire inside the same debounce reset cycle and waste CPU.

claudeteam.showBackgroundCount.description: Show a count of unrostered (background) agents in the dashboard.

claudeteam.showAllSessionsGlobally.description: Show Claude Code sessions from all workspaces, not just the current VS Code window. Default: false (only sessions whose cwd matches an open workspace folder are shown).

claudeteam.collapsePersonaTiles.description: When the same rostered persona (e.g. Felix) has been dispatched multiple times in a session, collapse the N tiles into one 'Felix ×N' header with an expandable list. Default: true. Set false to render every dispatch as its own tile (pre-M3-10 behavior).

claudeteam.hideFinishedAgents.description: Hide rostered agent tiles whose state is 'finished'. When true, the dashboard suppresses finished tiles and shows a count chip in the header (e.g. '3 finished hidden — show'). Background-noise rows are not affected — they collapse via the background chip instead. Toggle via Settings or the dashboard header chip.

claudeteam.diagnostic.verbose.description: Enable verbose per-tick diagnostic logging to the 'Claude Team — Diagnostics' Output channel. Emits one line per watcher tick (timestamp + duration + emitted flag), plus per-agent state-transition lines (running → idle → finished), roster reload events, and watcher errors. Default: false (silent). Toggle takes effect within one tick — no Reload Window required. Use for diagnosing why a tile is stuck in a given state or why an emit isn't reaching the webview.

scripts.test:vscode: npm run build && npm run compile:vscode-integration && node out/vscode-integration/tests/vscode-integration/runTest.js
